chief_complaint: A 70-year-old woman presented with refractory sinusitis and a persistent cough.
history: The patient had a past medical history of atrial fibrillation, sick sinus syndrome, breast cancer, gastroesophageal reflux disease, and anxiety. She developed iron-deficiency anemia and a rapid decline in renal function over 5 months.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not explicitly mentioned.
labs: Serologic testing revealed dual positivity for anti-proteinase 3 (PR3, c-ANCA) and anti-myeloperoxidase (MPO, p-ANCA). Renal biopsy confirmed pauci-immune glomerulonephritis.
differentials: Multisystem involvement due to SARS-CoV-2 infection
correct_ranking: Multisystem involvement due to SARS-CoV-2 infection
